title: Unable to expose ports when adding rootless container(s) to a pod
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am unable to expose ports when adding container(s) to a pod.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```console
$ podman pod create --name testpod
739faa5af9c18245634de958abb756c70777c3cb833278241c31cecac1a79b2c
$ podman run -dt --pod testpod -p 6379:6379 --name testredis redis                                          
Error: cannot set port bindings on an existing container network namespace
```

**Describe the results you received:**

I receive an error that podman cannot set port bindings on an existing container network namespace.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected to be able to set port bindings when adding a container to a pod.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Happens every time.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.9.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         d985723506a29766ed21585ba8541033db6bd572
Built:              Sat Apr 18 16:20:35 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: For now, ports must be exposed at pod creation time. There's been some talk in the past of changing this so new containers added to the pod would have their ports exposed after a pod restart, but discussions on this stalled on implementation details.

> For now, ports must be exposed at pod creation time.

Ah, yes so I just expose all the ports I think I'll need when creating the pod and then they are forwarded to all containers:

```console
$ podman pod create --name testpod -p 13306:3306 -p 16379:6379
$ podman run -dt --pod testpod -P --name testredis redis                                          
$ podman run -dt --pod testpod --name testmariadb -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=my-secret-pw mariadb:10.2
```

Yes this works for me for now and I am able to connect to services running in both, though I dunno what happens if there are multiple containers in the same pod listening on the same port.
